textPlain:
Very interesting. Also I find these training parameters quite elegant:- Diversity: This term encourages the model to generate a diverse set of samples, preventing mode collapse.
- Fidelity: This term rewards the model for making predictions that are close to the ground-truthI'm wondering if a continuos next-vector generative approach  also increase innate "reasoning" capabilities of the model, since it could potentially capture more of the semantics of the data vs just tokens.
And may be even more adapted to sorts of RL finetuning?
